summary: Completed testing of Iteration 14 features: Nordigen Open Banking, SendGrid Email, and WhatsApp Cloud API integrations. All 36 backend tests passed (100%). All three new /status endpoints correctly return configured: false with appropriate MOCKED messages since API keys are not configured.

minor.issue: Duplicate route definition - server.py (uses phone_number, requires auth) and whatsapp_routes.py (uses phone, no auth). Server.py route takes precedence.

passed_tests: GET /api/banking/status shows configured: false with Nordigen provider
GET /api/email/status shows configured: false with SendGrid provider
GET /api/whatsapp/status shows configured: false with WhatsApp Cloud API provider
GET /api/ returns MANO API welcome message
GET /api/auth/me returns user data with session cookie
All plan prices are correct (weekly €9.99, monthly €29.99, etc.)
GET /api/banking/institutions/{country} returns error when not configured
GET /api/banking/accounts returns empty list for authenticated user
critical_code_review_comments: Banking routes (/api/banking/*) correctly implemented with Nordigen client
Email routes (/api/email/*) correctly implemented with SendGrid templates
WhatsApp routes (/api/whatsapp/*) correctly implemented with Cloud API
All /status endpoints correctly return configured: false when API keys missing
MOCKED mode works correctly - emails and WhatsApp messages are queued but not sent
Minor: Duplicate /api/whatsapp/send route in server.py and whatsapp_routes.py - server.py takes precedence
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - tests use API endpoints directly
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 14 features fully tested. Three new integration endpoints added: /api/banking/status (Nordigen), /api/email/status (SendGrid), /api/whatsapp/status (WhatsApp Cloud API). All return configured: false since API keys are not set. Email and WhatsApp send endpoints work in MOCKED mode - messages are queued but not actually sent. Banking endpoints require auth and return 503/520 when trying to access institutions without API keys. Test user has 'investor' role, not 'superadmin', so admin routes return 403. Previous routes (auth, payments, threats) continue to work correctly.
